implemented: Authored crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_module_function_integ_test.rs with 21 named integration tests covering Ruby/Elixir/OCaml Module.function shapes (6 + 7 + 5 + 3 string-literal regression-guards), augmented crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_baseline_per_language_test.rs with 3 baseline tests, and added an analyze_ast_only(src, lang, fn_name) helper to crates/tldr-core/tests/common/integ_helpers.rs. The harness is implemented via a thread-local AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE Cell + a public AstOnlyTestModeGuard RAII type added to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs. While the guard is alive, detect_sources / detect_sinks short-circuit to empty Vec — sanitizer paths (detect_sanitizer / detect_sanitizer_ast) are unaffected. Mirrors the W2-pre 'AST-only mode simulation' pattern (W2-pre-report.json:45). Each Module.function fixture asserts under BOTH the regular `analyze_with_ssa` path AND the `analyze_ast_only` path.

harness_mechanism.approach_chosen: Option D (custom — thread-local AST-only-mode flag + public RAII guard)
harness_mechanism.rationale: Plan §M1 Option A (transient bank-empty by mutating LanguagePatterns) was infeasible: the lazy_static blocks produce &'static LanguagePatterns and there is no swap-out mechanism. Option B (compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns alternative entry) would require +200 LOC of duplicated logic (compute_taint_with_tree is 331 lines). Option C (call detect_sources_ast / detect_sinks_ast directly) bypasses the dedup/flow-construction logic and would diverge from production behavior. Chosen: thread-local Cell<bool> + AstOnlyTestModeGuard (RAII restore-on-Drop) + early-return in detect_sources / detect_sinks (lines 802-810 and 911-919 of taint.rs). The flag is process-local to a single thread and the guard restores the previous value on Drop, so concurrent tests on different threads do not see each other's overrides and mid-test panics still restore the flag. Production code never sets the flag — runtime cost is one Cell::get() on each detect_sources / detect_sinks entry, negligible.
harness_mechanism.production_behavior_change: None. The thread-local defaults to false; only test code that constructs an AstOnlyTestModeGuard activates the bypass. detect_sanitizer / detect_sanitizer_ast paths untouched.

tests.rr_module_function_integ_test_summary: 21 total: 18 PASSED, 3 FAILED. 3 FAILED comprises (a) ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint AST-only path FAILED — legitimate RED gate (bare `gets` in Ruby is parsed as identifier not call_expression, so AST-only with regex bank empty produces no UserInput source; this is a Ruby grammar / call_names-coverage gap, NOT a Module.function shape gap M2 addresses); (b) elixir_string_literal_system_cmd_substring_zero_findings REGULAR path FAILED — pre-existing bug; (c) ocaml_string_literal_sys_command_substring_zero_findings REGULAR path FAILED — pre-existing bug.
tests.rr_baseline_per_language_test_summary: 19 total: 19 PASSED including the 3 newly added (ruby_module_function_io_popen_baseline, elixir_module_function_system_cmd_baseline, ocaml_module_function_sys_command_baseline). Captured to reports/M1-baseline-capture.txt.
tests.regression_guard_summary: Existing rr_framework_integ_test (18 PASS), rr_stdlib_integ_test (PASS), val001a_taint_substring_shadowing_test (PASS), val001b_ssa_versioned_taint_test (PASS), val002_member_access_structural_test (5 PASS), val002_cfg_break_ssa_params_test (2 PASS), val003_taint_ts_frameworks_test (4 PASS), security::taint_tests::* (70 lib tests PASS). No regressions introduced by the harness or the new tests.
tests.red_state_summary: The plan-predicted RED gate (all 18 Module.function fixtures FAIL under analyze_ast_only at HEAD) DID NOT MATERIALIZE. Of 18 Module.function tests under analyze_ast_only, 17 PASS at HEAD. See discoveries.architectural_finding_1. The 1 legitimate RED gate (ruby_io_popen_with_user_input AST-only) FAILS for an orthogonal reason (bare-call gap in Ruby), not the planned Module.function-rewrite gap.

checks.result: 70 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored — including all 6 obsolete-marked tests (test_ruby_detect_sources, test_ruby_detect_sinks, test_elixir_detect_sources, test_elixir_detect_sinks, test_ocaml_detect_sources, test_ocaml_detect_sinks) still GREEN at HEAD.

discoveries.title: Plan §M1's RED-gate prediction was incorrect — raw-substring fallback at member_patterns_match catches Module.function shapes under AST-only
discoveries.details: The plan predicted that analyze_ast_only would FAIL at HEAD for all 18 Module.function fixtures because raw-substring `('', 'X.y')` AST entries are skipped on the structural and W2-pre call-shape paths. Investigation of taint.rs reveals a THIRD path the plan missed: a raw-substring fallback at taint.rs:3088-3092 inside member_patterns_match that does `descendant_text.contains(pat_field)` for entries with empty receiver. This fallback handles `('', 'IO.popen')`, `('', 'System.cmd')`, etc. directly off the AST descendant text — it does NOT depend on the regex bank. Consequence: with regex banks emptied (analyze_ast_only), 17 of 18 Module.function fixtures PASS at HEAD because the raw-substring fallback catches them.
discoveries.evidence: rr_module_function_integ_test results show ruby_stdin_read/gets/readline/file_read/file_open, elixir_io_gets/system_get_env/file_read/file_read_bang/user_input_*/pipe_operator, ocaml_sys_getenv/in_channel_*/read_line/user_input_to_sqlite3 ALL PASS under analyze_ast_only (17/18 Module.function fixtures pass).
discoveries.implication_for_m2_m3_m4: The structured-shape rewrites in M2/M3/M4 are NOT behavior-changing at the test level — they are PREP for M5. Until M5 deletes both (a) the regex banks AND (b) the raw-fallback duplicate AST entries, the structured rewrites are functionally additive (idempotent overlap with the raw-fallback path). M5 is the only milestone where structured-shape entries become load-bearing. M1 RED gate should be re-framed as 'M5 RED gate' — meaningful tests for the structured-rewrites would need to bypass BOTH the regex banks AND the raw-substring fallback at taint.rs:3088. Implementing such a bypass would require modifying member_patterns_match runtime behavior, which exceeds M1 scope and would compromise the harness's role as a test-only switch.
discoveries.recommendation: Per the prompt's escalation guidance, this is a case to flag rather than silently fix. The dual-path harness IS real (verified by the 1 legitimate AST-only RED at ruby_io_popen_with_user_input). The plan-predicted ubiquitous RED state simply doesn't exist due to the architectural reality at taint.rs:3088. Orchestrator should consider amending the plan: (a) re-scope M1's RED gate to the 1 fixture that actually goes RED + the 3 string-literal pre-existing bugs; (b) re-frame M2/M3/M4 STOP thresholds from 'fixtures transition RED→GREEN under analyze_ast_only' to 'cargo check passes; existing tests stay GREEN; structured entries added' (purely additive); (c) move the load-bearing RED gate to M5 — assert that AFTER raw-fallback duplicate deletion, all 18 fixtures still PASS under both paths (proving structured shape is sufficient).

discoveries.title: Ruby bare `gets` is not detected via AST call_names path — it's parsed as identifier, not call_expression
discoveries.details: ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint AST-only path FAILS with `[ast_only] expected at least one UserInput source; got sources=[]`. The fixture is `cmd = gets; IO.popen(cmd)`. Bare `gets` in tree-sitter-ruby is parsed as an identifier (or method_call without explicit receiver) — `call_kinds.contains(&descendant.kind())` doesn't fire for it on this descendant kind. The regex bank `\bgets\b` catches it under regular dispatch but not under AST-only. This is a pre-existing under-coverage in RUBY_AST_SOURCES `call_names: ['gets']` — the entry is correctly listed but the AST grammar doesn't deliver `gets` to the call_kinds path. Out of scope for field_access_info-extension-v1 (this milestone is Module.function, not bare-call). Documented as a known limitation; M5 will leave bare-`gets` covered via the regex bank's `\bgets\b` source pattern OR via subsequent hardening. Note: plan.md §3 Ruby risk register mentions bare `gets` is handled via `call_names: ['gets']`, but my tests reveal that's only true under regular dispatch — under AST-only the call_kinds gate filters it out.
discoveries.implication_for_m5: M5 plans to delete `\bgets\b` from RUBY_PATTERNS.sources. This will regress ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint's REGULAR path — both paths will then fail. Recommendation: M5 should either (a) keep `\bgets\b` regex (defer bare-call AST coverage to a future milestone), OR (b) also add a Ruby-specific pre-call_kinds AST descendant kind to the call_kinds list, OR (c) add `('*', 'gets')` wildcard match. Verify Ruby tree-sitter parse of bare `gets` and choose accordingly during M5 planning.

discoveries.title: Pre-existing string-literal false-positives in regex banks (closes-#24 generalization)
discoveries.details: Two of three string-literal regression-guard tests FAIL on the REGULAR path at HEAD: (a) elixir_string_literal_system_cmd_substring_zero_findings — fixture `def handler do; msg = "System.cmd is dangerous"; IO.puts(msg); end` produces a ShellExec sink with var='handler' line=1, statement='def handler do'. The AST raw-substring fallback at taint.rs:3088 hits because the outer function-def descendant text contains `System.cmd`. is_in_string filters the inner literal node but not the outer function-def descendant. (b) ocaml_string_literal_sys_command_substring_zero_findings — fixture produces ShellExec sink with var='is' line=2 from string `"Sys.command is dangerous"`. The OCaml regex `Sys\.command\s` matches the trailing space inside the string literal because regex-side detect_sinks doesn't filter string contexts.
discoveries.scope: Pre-existing — these failures are reproducible at HEAD without any of M1's changes (verified by running with regex banks at HEAD; the AST raw-substring fallback was added in regex-removal-v1 W2-pre at taint.rs:3088). NOT introduced by M1.
discoveries.implication_for_m5: These will likely transition GREEN under analyze (regex bank deleted) but stay RED under analyze_ast_only (AST raw-fallback still present until raw-substring duplicates are deleted in M5). Once M5 deletes the raw-fallback `('', 'X.y')` duplicates AND the regex banks, both paths converge to the structured-only shape which (correctly) doesn't fire on string literals. So M5 will fix these regressions as a side-effect of its main deletion. M5 STOP threshold should explicitly verify these 3 tests transition GREEN.

discoveries.title: ruby_string_literal_io_popen_substring_zero_findings PASSES at HEAD — Ruby's regex bank doesn't have the same string-leak bug as Elixir/OCaml
discoveries.details: Curiously, the Ruby string-literal regression-guard PASSES at HEAD on both paths. Examining RUBY_PATTERNS.sinks shows IO.popen regex is `IO\.popen\s*\(` (requires open-paren). The string fixture `"do not run IO.popen here"` lacks `(`, so the regex doesn't match. AST raw-fallback `descendant_text.contains('IO.popen')` would still hit for the outer function-def descendant — yet the test PASSES. This suggests Ruby's tree-sitter grammar walks descendants differently or the outer `method` def descendant is filtered out earlier. Did not investigate further (out of scope); flagged for M5 STOP threshold to monitor.

issues: rr_module_function_integ_test currently exits non-zero (3 of 21 tests fail). Per the prompt's instructions ('If the AST-only fails are properly RED (panic at the assertion), this is the correct gating signal for M2/M3/M4'), these RED tests are committed AS-IS to serve as the gating signal — but the gating semantics need re-framing per architectural_finding_1. Orchestrator may want to amend M2/M3/M4 STOP thresholds before launching those workers.

files_per_change.purpose: thread-local AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE + AstOnlyTestModeGuard RAII type + early-return in detect_sources / detect_sinks
21 named integration tests + dual-path RED protocol

compliance.taint_rs_modification: Yes — added thread-local + RAII guard + early-return gates in detect_sources/detect_sinks (72 LOC). The harness mechanism could not be implemented purely in the test crate because (a) lazy_static produces &'static immutable refs, (b) compute_taint_with_tree is 331 lines and a parallel entry point would be a 200+ LOC duplication. Per prompt: 'If you find that simulating AST-only requires a test-only crate-private function, it's acceptable to add ONE such helper to taint.rs — but mark it #[cfg(test)] so it doesn't ship in release builds. Document this in your report's discoveries.' — the AstOnlyTestModeGuard is technically NOT cfg(test) gated because integration tests in tests/ are not in the same compilation unit as the lib (cfg(test) inside src/ does not apply to integration tests linking against the lib as a normal dependency). The thread-local defaults to false in production and is only ever set by AstOnlyTestModeGuard::enter() — which is only called from analyze_ast_only in test code. Production runtime overhead: one Cell::get() per detect_sources/detect_sinks entry. This is the minimal-impact mechanism that satisfies both the test-only requirement and the no-fake-harness requirement.
